cot_prefix: Okay, let's read this carefully. The story is about Mr. Williams, a careful driver. He comes to a crossroad with a "SLOW" sign. He doesn't completely stop, just slows down, looks both ways and drives across. Then he hears a police whistle and stops. The policeman says he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams argues that the sign said "SLOW," not "STOP," and he did slow down. The policeman realizes he's in the wrong street. The question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The options are: (A) was driving at a high speed (B) was driving onto the main road (C) was going to stop his car (D) was driving slowly Let me break it down. The key is what he was doing when stopped. According
